Title: OsCK2 Phosphorylates OsSIZ1 to Regulate Phosphate Starvation Response and Defence Response in Rice

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 mutant analysis; qRT-PCR of Pi starvation-induced genes; anti-SUMO1 immunoblotting; yeast two-hybrid; GST pull-down; co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; transcriptome RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoots and roots for phosphate; shoots for defence-related diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phosphate starvation response; phosphate homeostasis; defence-related diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis via OsSIZ1-dependent transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: phosphorylation-mediated regulation and protein stabilization control of OsSIZ1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly represses Pi starvation-induced genes and defence genes under Pi-sufficient conditions via OsSIZ1; mutation induces OsPT2; OsPT8; OsPAP21b and co-induces defence-related genes including OsCPS2; OsCPS4; OsCYP99A3; OsKSL4; OsKSL7; OsKSL8; OsKSL10; OsMAS1; OsMAS2; OsPR genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in OsCK2 holoenzyme with OsCK2α3; OsCK2α3 interaction with OsSIZ1 requires OsCK2β3 in planta
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR mutation of OsCK2β3 caused Pi accumulation in shoots and roots and altered Pi starvation responses under Pi-sufficient conditions. 2. OsCK2β3 physically interacted with OsSIZ1 and enabled the OsCK2 holoenzyme to phosphorylate OsSIZ1. 3. Osck2β3 mutants showed reduced global SUMOylation and transcriptome changes overlapping strongly with Ossiz1, especially induction of diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis and pathogenesis-related genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsCK2β3 was identified as the beta subunit linking CK2 to nuclear regulation of OsSIZ1; previous studies on OsPHT1 and OsPHO2 are background rather than core focus here.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream controller that links Pi supply to OsSIZ1 phosphorylation and thereby suppresses phosphate starvation and defence outputs under Pi-sufficient conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Osck2β3 mutants displayed growth defects under Pi-sufficient conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphate is an essential mineral nutrient for plant growth; diterpenoid phytoalexins contribute to resistance against Magnaporthe oryzae, Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct knockout has growth penalty, but the module is valuable as an upstream target to engineer Pi efficiency and disease resistance through downstream OsSIZ1 phosphosite manipulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsCK2β3 increases Pi accumulation and activates defence-associated transcriptional programs while causing growth defects.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SAP-PHD-PINIT-SR-RING SUMO E3 ligase with SIM domains
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 mutant analysis; overexpression of OsSIZ1 and phospho-deficient OsSIZ1AA; qRT-PCR; anti-SUMO1 immunoblotting; protein stability assay; cell-free degradation assay; RNA-seq; pathogen infection assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoots and roots for phosphate; shoots for defence-related diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phosphate starvation response; phosphate homeostasis; defence response gene expression; global SUMOylation status
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulation of Pi starvation and defence responses; phosphorylation-dependent protein stability control by OsCK2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates expression of OsPT2; OsPT8; OsPAP21b and defence genes including OsCPS2; OsCPS4; OsCYP99A3; OsKSL4; OsKSL10; OsPR10b under Pi-sufficient conditions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes involved in Pi uptake/starvation response and diterpenoid phytoalexin/pathogenesis-related pathways
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with OsCK2β3 and OsCK2α3-containing OsCK2 holoenzyme; interacts with OsSUMO1 in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ossiz1 mutants accumulated more Pi and showed altered Pi starvation responses, including induction of OsPT2, OsPT8 and OsPAP21b. 2. OsSIZ1 directly interacts with OsCK2 and is phosphorylated at S607 and S609, affecting protein stability in response to Pi. 3. Loss of OsSIZ1 function or expression of non-phosphorylatable OsSIZ1AA induces defence-related genes, enhances resistance to multiple pathogens, and OsSIZ1AA improves phosphorus acquisition without growth penalty.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pi-sufficient versus Pi-deficient hydroponic culture; infection with Magnaporthe oryzae, Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsSIZ1 is the central output node of the OsCK2-OsSIZ1 module; phosphosite mutation S607A/S609A does not alter nuclear localization or OsSUMO1 binding in yeast but changes stability and biological output.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major condition-responsive negative regulator whose reduced or altered phosphorylation state promotes phosphate acquisition and defence-related phytoalexin pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CRISPR loss-of-function mutants show reduced growth/fresh weight; native overexpression suppresses growth, whereas OsSIZ1AA overexpression avoids this penalty.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphate is essential for plant growth; diterpenoid phytoalexins function in defence against fungal and bacterial pathogens.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter for OsSIZ1-Flag and OsSIZ1AA-Flag overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value target for phosphosite engineering; non-phosphorylatable OsSIZ1AA is a promising allele to improve phosphorus acquisition and broad-spectrum disease resistance without growth defects.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pi supply status via OsCK2-mediated phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsSIZ1 negatively regulates Pi acquisition and defence responses; phospho-deficient OsSIZ1AA enhances Pi accumulation and pathogen resistance while maintaining normal growth.
